title: Why Many Smokers Are Wrong About Vaping Risks
subtitle: New research shows that public confusion about e-cigarettes is preventing smokers from quitting tobacco.

summary.en: A new study reveals that over half of UK smokers wrongly believe vaping is as dangerous as smoking cigarettes. Experts warn that this misunderstanding stops people from switching to a less harmful alternative. Research from King’s College London confirms that vapes expose users to far fewer toxic substances than tobacco. Media coverage focusing on vaping risks without comparing them to smoking is identified as a major cause of this confusion. Health officials are now calling for better public information to help smokers quit successfully.
summary.zh: 一項最新研究顯示，超過半數的英國吸菸者錯誤地認為電子煙與紙菸同樣危險。專家警告，這種誤解阻礙了民眾轉向危害較小的替代品。倫敦國王學院的研究證實，電子煙使用者接觸到的有毒物質遠少於菸草。媒體在報導電子煙風險時，若未與紙菸進行對比，被視為造成此類混淆的主因。衛生官員目前正呼籲提供更完善的公共資訊，以協助吸菸者成功戒菸。

paragraphs.en: Following yesterday’s news that the NHS is using new technology to improve patient care, health experts are highlighting another urgent issue: a growing misunderstanding about vaping. New research from Action on Smoking and Health (Ash) shows that more than half of adult smokers in the UK wrongly believe that vaping is just as harmful, or even more harmful, than smoking traditional cigarettes. This confusion is a serious problem because it discourages smokers from switching to a much safer way to get nicotine.
paragraphs.zh: 繼昨日英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）宣布運用新科技提升病患照護品質的消息後，健康專家們正聚焦於另一項迫切議題：大眾對電子煙日益嚴重的誤解。根據「吸菸與健康行動組織」（Ash）的最新研究顯示，超過半數的英國成年吸菸者錯誤地認為，使用電子煙與吸食傳統紙菸一樣有害，甚至危害更大。這種認知混淆是一個嚴重的問題，因為它阻礙了吸菸者轉而選擇一種獲取尼古丁且安全得多的方式。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, this belief has become much more common over the last ten years. A decade ago, only about a quarter of adults held this view, but that number has now doubled. The study, which surveyed over 13,000 adults, found that 52% of smokers think vaping is as dangerous as smoking. Even more concerning, 61% of smokers who have never tried a vape believe it is just as bad for their health. Because of these fears, some people have even stopped vaping and gone back to smoking cigarettes, which is a much more dangerous habit.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，這種觀念在過去十年間變得愈發普遍。十年前，僅約四分之一的成年人持有此看法，但如今該比例已翻倍。這項調查涵蓋超過一萬三千名成年人，結果發現百分之五十二的吸菸者認為電子煙與紙菸同樣危險。更令人擔憂的是，百分之六十一從未嘗試過電子煙的吸菸者也認為它對健康同樣有害。由於這些恐懼，有些人甚至停止使用電子煙，轉而回頭吸食危害程度高出許多的紙菸。

paragraphs.en: Scientific evidence shows that while vaping is not completely risk-free, it is far less harmful than smoking tobacco. Tobacco smoke contains thousands of chemicals, including toxic metals and poisonous gases that are known to cause cancer. In contrast, a major review by King’s College London found that e-cigarettes pose only a fraction of the risk of tobacco. Vapers are significantly less exposed to the harmful substances found in traditional cigarettes, making vaping an effective tool for those trying to quit.
paragraphs.zh: 科學證據顯示，儘管電子煙並非完全沒有風險，但其危害遠低於菸草。菸草燃燒後的煙霧含有數千種化學物質，包括已知會致癌的有毒金屬與毒氣。相比之下，倫敦國王學院（King’s College London）的一項大型審查研究發現，電子煙所帶來的風險僅為菸草的一小部分。電子煙使用者接觸到傳統紙菸中有害物質的程度顯著較低，這使得電子煙成為戒菸者的一項有效工具。

paragraphs.en: Why has public opinion changed so much? Experts suggest that the way the media reports on health studies is a major factor. Prof Jamie Brown, director of the UCL Tobacco and Alcohol Research Group, noted that many news stories focus on the potential risks of vaping without explaining how much worse cigarettes are. When people see headlines about the dangers of vaping without any comparison to the deadly effects of tobacco, they naturally assume that both are equally bad. This lack of context creates a false impression that prevents smokers from choosing a better path.
paragraphs.zh: 為何大眾觀感會有如此大的轉變？專家指出，媒體報導健康研究的方式是主要因素。倫敦大學學院（UCL）菸酒研究小組主任布朗教授（Prof Jamie Brown）指出，許多新聞報導過度聚焦於電子煙的潛在風險，卻未說明紙菸的危害嚴重得多。當民眾看到關於電子煙危險性的標題，卻缺乏與菸草致命影響的對比時，他們自然會假設兩者同樣糟糕。這種缺乏背景資訊的情況造成了錯誤印象，阻礙了吸菸者選擇更好的戒菸途徑。

paragraphs.en: There is also a concern that some people are intentionally spreading fear. Prof Peter Hajek from Queen Mary University of London suggested that some anti-smoking advocates believe it is acceptable to exaggerate the risks of vaping to discourage people from using any nicotine products at all. However, experts argue that this approach is unethical and counterproductive. By making smokers afraid of vapes, these messages effectively close the door to a relatively easy way for people to stop smoking.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，也有人擔心部分人士正刻意散布恐懼。倫敦瑪麗皇后大學（Queen Mary University of London）的海耶克教授（Prof Peter Hajek）認為，一些反菸倡議者認為，為了勸阻民眾使用任何尼古丁產品，誇大電子煙的風險是可以接受的。然而，專家主張這種做法既不道德且適得其反。這些訊息讓吸菸者對電子煙產生恐懼，實際上等於關閉了一條相對容易的戒菸之路。

paragraphs.en: Hazel Cheeseman, the chief executive of Ash, explained that these misconceptions have real-life consequences. When smokers believe that vaping offers no health benefit, they are less likely to try it as a way to quit. This is a significant public health issue because two out of three long-term smokers will die from a smoking-related illness if they do not stop. For these individuals, switching to a vape could be a life-saving step.
paragraphs.zh: 「吸菸與健康行動組織」執行長奇斯曼（Hazel Cheeseman）解釋，這些誤解會造成現實生活中的後果。當吸菸者認為電子煙對健康毫無益處時，他們嘗試將其作為戒菸手段的意願就會降低。這是一個重大的公共衛生問題，因為若不戒菸，三分之二的長期吸菸者將死於與吸菸相關的疾病。對於這些人而言，改用電子煙可能是救命的一步。

paragraphs.en: The situation is particularly worrying because of how people are choosing to quit. The survey found that nearly one in five people who tried to stop vaping actually switched to cigarettes instead. This is the opposite of what health professionals want to see. As the government prepares to introduce new rules for tobacco and vaping products, experts are calling for better communication to ensure the public understands the difference in risk between the two.
paragraphs.zh: 由於民眾選擇戒菸的方式，情況顯得格外令人擔憂。調查發現，近五分之一嘗試戒除電子煙的人，實際上轉而改吸紙菸。這與醫療專業人員所期望的結果背道而馳。隨著政府準備針對菸草與電子煙產品引入新法規，專家們正呼籲加強溝通，確保大眾了解兩者在風險程度上的差異。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the goal of public health officials is to help as many people as possible give up cigarettes. With the NHS already working to improve patient services through new digital tools, experts hope that clear, accurate information about vaping will be the next step in reducing the number of people suffering from smoking-related diseases. Correcting these false beliefs is now considered an urgent priority for doctors and health organizations across the country.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，公共衛生官員的目標是協助盡可能多的人戒除紙菸。隨著英國國家醫療保健服務正致力於透過數位工具改善病患服務，專家們希望關於電子煙清晰且準確的資訊，能成為減少菸害疾病患者人數的下一步。糾正這些錯誤觀念，目前已被視為全國醫師與衛生組織的當務之急。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Changing from one thing to another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（開關），這裡作動詞的現在分詞，指改用電子菸。文中：This confusion is a serious problem because it discourages smokers from switching to a much safer way to get nicotine.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To present or constitute (a problem, danger, or risk).
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（姿勢），這裡作動詞，指造成風險。文中：In contrast, a major review by King’s College London found that e-cigarettes pose only a fraction of the risk of tobacco.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make something impossible or prevent an opportunity from happening.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 斷絕……的機會、關閉……的大門。
keyTerms.noteZh: 非字面意思，指阻斷了某種可能性。文中：By making smokers afraid of vapes, these messages effectively close the door to a relatively easy way for people to stop smoking.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Majority of UK smokers wrongly believe vaping is as harmful as cigarettes, experts find

previousArticles.title: NHS Expands AI Tools to Improve Patient Care and Reduce Wait Times
previousArticles.summaryEn: The NHS is launching a new AI tool on its mobile app to help patients find the right medical services. This system will triage patients, directing them to pharmacies or A&E if they do not need a GP appointment. The project is part of a large £10bn investment to update technology across the health service. While officials believe this will save time, some health experts are concerned about privacy and the lack of a clear long-term strategy. The government hopes these changes will eventually end the difficult morning rush for same-day appointments.

quiz.question: According to the survey mentioned in the article, what percentage of smokers who have never used a vape believe it is just as harmful as smoking?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第二段，研究發現 61% 從未嘗試過電子菸的吸菸者認為電子菸與傳統香菸一樣有害。選項 A 是十年前認為電子菸有害的比例，非現今數據；選項 B 是所有受訪吸菸者認為電子菸有害的比例（52%）；選項 D 則是提到改回吸菸的人數比例，皆非題目所問。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might some anti-smoking advocates be intentionally exaggerating the risks of vaping?

options.A: They believe that all nicotine products are equally addictive.
options.B: They want to discourage people from using any form of nicotine, regardless of the health trade-off.
options.C: They are trying to support the NHS in its new digital health initiatives.
options.D: They have scientific evidence that vaping is more dangerous than traditional tobacco.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第五段，專家指出部分反菸人士認為誇大電子菸風險是可接受的，目的是為了讓大眾完全放棄使用任何尼古丁產品。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 與文章提到的 NHS 數位化目標無關；選項 D 與文章強調電子菸風險遠低於香菸的科學證據直接矛盾。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'counterproductive' mean as it is used in the context of exaggerating the risks of vaping?

options.A: Having an effect that is the opposite of what was intended.
options.B: Being extremely expensive and difficult to maintain.
options.C: Providing a highly effective way to solve a problem.
options.D: Being scientifically proven to be accurate and reliable.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到專家認為誇大風險是「counterproductive」，因為這反而讓吸菸者不敢嘗試電子菸，導致他們繼續抽傳統香菸，這與反菸人士希望大眾戒菸的初衷背道而馳。因此，該詞意指「適得其反」。其他選項皆與語境中描述的負面影響不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message the author wants to convey regarding public perception of vaping?

options.A: Vaping is completely safe and should be encouraged for everyone, including non-smokers.
options.B: The media is the only reason why people are confused about the dangers of vaping.
options.C: Misinformation about the risks of vaping is preventing smokers from switching to a less harmful alternative.
options.D: The government needs to ban both traditional cigarettes and e-cigarettes immediately to protect public health.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心觀點在於指出大眾對於電子菸的誤解（認為其與香菸一樣有害）正在阻礙吸菸者轉向較安全的替代品，進而影響公共衛生。選項 A 過度誇大電子菸的安全性；選項 B 忽略了反菸人士的影響；選項 D 並非文章建議的解決方案，文章強調的是需要更準確的溝通與資訊傳遞。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
